cards.title: Suavizar el feedback
cards.lede: En inglés americano, el feedback directo suena brusco. Usa estas fórmulas para que tus comentarios sean profesionales sin perder claridad.

rows.value: It might be worth extracting this into a helper.
I wonder if we could cache this result.
Have you considered using a Map here?
This could be simplified to a single reduce call.
Nit: variable name could be more descriptive.

tip.label: Registro:
tip.body: Estos hedges son normales en PR comments americanos — no son debilidad, son colaboración.

cards.title: LGTM y más allá
cards.lede: Hay muchas formas de aprobar un PR sin sonar mecánico.

rows.value: Looks good to me — nice work!
Good catch — I hadn't thought of that edge case.
This is clean and easy to follow.
I like how you separated concerns here.

cards.title: Explicar tu approach
cards.lede: Cuando te preguntan por qué hiciste algo, estas frases te dan estructura y suenan seguras sin ser defensivas.

rows.value: I went with a queue because it decouples the producer from the consumer.
The trade-off here is simplicity vs. performance.
I can change it to a factory if that's clearer.
You're right, I'll fix that in the next commit.

cards.title: El vocabulario del día a día
cards.lede: Estas frases suenan naturales en conversaciones técnicas en inglés americano.

rows.value: We can deploy to production once the tests pass.
If something breaks, we can roll back the change.
I want to push back on the deadline — we need more time.
Let's ship the feature behind a flag first.
I'll address your comment before merging.

cards.title: Hablar en el daily
cards.lede: Reportar progreso en inglés requiere los tiempos perfectos — así suenas fluido, no traducido.

lines.text: Yesterday I worked on the login.
I've been working on the login flow — almost done.
I have a blocker with the API.
I'm blocked on the API rate limits — could use some help.
I will review your PR today.
I'll take a look at your PR this morning.
